data.content:
The build confirms the file is generated (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler can't find the `TestProject.Generated` namespace — **CS0246**.

**Root cause:** The target writes the `.cs` file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing only evaluates at project load (evaluation time), before any targets run, so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`, but it doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after writing the file. This is the classic "including generated files" pattern in MSBuild.

The target needs to include:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

I should also register it with `FileWrites` so the `Clean` target properly removes it during clean builds.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Diagnosis & Fix:**

| Problem | MSBuild globs expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the file written by `GenerateSampleCode` was never seen by the compiler. |
|---|---|
| **Fix** | Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is included at **execution time**. Also added `<FileWrites>` so `dotnet clean` removes it. |
